alerts.title: Russia reportedly fires two 'Oreshnik' hypersonic IRBMs with MIRVs at Kiev region
alerts.summary: ME Spectator (~54K views) reports Russia launched two Oreshnik intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Ukraine, with footage showing multiple independently targetable reentry vehicles (MIRVs) impacting the Kiev region. The platform is normally configured for nuclear payloads and is described as nearly impossible to intercept; Rybar separately confirms overnight strikes hit Kiev's Solomenskyi district, the Lahoda business park and an aviation plant, plus a fuel depot in Myrhorod.

alerts.title: US official confirms Washington and Tehran agree in principle to reopen Strait of Hormuz
alerts.summary: The New York Times, citing a US official, reports the United States and Iran have reached an in-principle agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, with DW and BBC echoing an 'upbeat tone' on both sides. The Times of Israel adds the framework reportedly bars US and allied strikes on Iran and its proxies, and vice versa — a sweeping non-attack clause beyond shipping access.

alerts.title: Israeli intelligence warns Tehran may be preparing surprise attack amid talks
alerts.summary: Jerusalem Post cites intelligence officials warning that Iran could be planning a surprise attack even as Hormuz/non-attack diplomacy advances, while Fortune reports the generous US terms are themselves making Tehran more suspicious of a setup. The warning lands as threat level holds at 9/10 (CRITICAL) and breakout time remains ~1-2 weeks.

alerts.title: US deploys defense and tech 'umbrella' to shield commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz
alerts.summary: DefenseScoop reports the US is deploying an integrated defense and technology 'umbrella' over shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, as ynetnews notes the US military build-up around Iran continues to 'test the balance of power' through the talks. USS Florida (SSGN) remains on station in the Persian Gulf alongside Red Sea carrier and amphibious groups (Eisenhower, Bataan, Carney, Mason, Laboon).

alerts.title: Israeli strikes kill Gaza infant, parents and five police officers in central Gaza
alerts.summary: Reuters, Al Jazeera and The National report Israeli strikes in central Gaza killed a six-month-old boy and his parents, with a separate Reuters dispatch citing Gaza police saying five officers were also killed in a post-warning strike on a residential building. The Guardian additionally reports Israeli bombardment reducing buildings to craters in southern Lebanon, indicating continued multi-front kinetic activity even as Iran diplomacy proceeds.

analysis_note: US-Iran diplomacy is advancing toward a formal Hormuz/non-attack framework with NYT-level confirmation, but Israeli intel warns of an Iranian sucker-punch, Russia has reportedly used MIRV-capable Oreshnik IRBMs against Kiev, and Israeli kinetic operations against Gaza and southern Lebanon continue unabated — keeping the threat board at CRITICAL despite the diplomatic track.
